head.hi: The Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs at Nanking to the Chinese Legation
head.note: Copy of telegram transmitted to the Department by the Chinese Legation, December 9, 1932.

div.p: Peiping message reports that General Chang Yu-Ting, Chinese Volunteer Commander, is now rallying scattered soldiers east of Hei-lungkiang and establishing contact with other Volunteer armies preparatory to a counterattack against Japanese forces. General Ma Chan-Shan also reports that the Chinese Volunteers at the front still exceed thirty thousand all of whom are determined to fight the Japanese in spite of the retreat of General Su Ping-Wen.
